grants.grant_title: BBSRC standard research grant: applicant-led mode
grants.funder_name: Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council
grants.description: Apply for funding for a research grant in any area within the remit of the Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council (BBSRC). You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for BBSRC funding.

grants.grant_title: BBSRC new investigator award: applicant-led mode
grants.funder_name: Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council
grants.description: Apply for funding for a research grant in any area within the remit of the Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council (BBSRC). You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for BBSRC funding.

grants.grant_title: MRC: new investigator research grant: applicant-led
grants.description: Apply for funding to take the next step towards becoming an independent researcher. Your research must be in the remit of the Medical Research Council. You must have: the skills and experience to ‘transition to independence’ the support of an eligible host research organisation

grants.grant_title: MRC: research grant: applicant-led
grants.description: Apply for funding to support exciting and ambitious research within the remit of the Medical Research Council, to transform our understanding of human health and disease You must be employed by an eligible research organisation. You can involve more than one research group or organisation in the project.

grants.grant_title: MRC: partnership grant: applicant-led
grants.description: Apply for funding to support exciting and ambitious partnerships within the remit of the Medical Research Council. You must be employed by an eligible research organisation. Partnerships are novel collaborative activities between researchers to transform our understanding of human health and disease.

grants.grant_title: Pushing the frontiers of environmental research
grants.description: Apply for funding to pursue an adventurous, ambitious, curiosity-driven project in environmental research. You must be: based at a UK research organisation eligible for Natural Environment Research Council (NERC) funding in a role that meets the individual eligibility requirements

grants.grant_title: Using the ‘Our Future Health’ data resource for research
grants.description: This is a pilot scheme through which the Medical Research Council (MRC) will cover data access costs for eligible researchers who are currently supported by MRC. Data is accessed through Our Future Health and you must apply for access through the ‘Our Future Health’ process.

grants.grant_title: Pre-announcement: NetworkPlus in property flood resilience
grants.funder_name: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council
grants.description: Apply for funding to develop an interdisciplinary NetworkPlus to address research needs for increased property flood resilience adoption and uptake in the UK. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for EPSRC funding.

grants.grant_title: UKRI Translation: AHRC Proof of Concept
grants.description: UKRI Translation: AHRC Proof of Concept supports arts and humanities activity that translate previously funded AHRC or UKRI projects into real world economic, societal, cultural or policy impact. This opportunity was previously known as AHRC responsive mode: follow-on funding for impact and engagement.

grants.grant_title: DRIVE35 Scale-up Fund
grants.description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £150 million to create scale up manufacturing facilities for zero emission vehicle technologies.

grants.grant_title: Advanced materials: highlight notice
grants.funder_name: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council
grants.description: EPSRC is seeking to support applications through our standard research grant scheme in the following two areas: advanced materials solutions for critical minerals in-situ and in operando materials metrology and characterisation You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for EPSRC funding.

grants.grant_title: Isambard-AI and Dawn AIRR supercomputers: Gateway route

grants.grant_title: Isambard-AI and Dawn AIRR supercomputers: Rapid Access route
grants.description: UK-registered micro, small and medium businesses can apply for 20,000 graphics processing unit hours on the Isambard-AI and Dawn AI Research Resource (AIRR) supercomputers for early-stage artificial intelligence product research and development.

grants.grant_title: UK International Ocean Drilling Programme moratorium awards
grants.description: Apply for funding to undertake research associated with an IODP3 expedition or SPARC project. You can apply for funding if you are invited and are a member of science parties for IODP3 drilling expeditions and SPARC projects. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for NERC funding.

grants.grant_title: Collaborate with researchers in Norway
grants.description: UKRI and Research Council of Norway have signed a Money Follows Cooperation agreement to enable collaborative applications between researchers in the UK and Norway. UKRI funding will be provided for project co-leads based in Norway in successful applications. Applications must be submitted through existing council programmes.

grants.grant_title: EPSRC Programme grant full proposal
grants.funder_name: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council
grants.description: Programme grants provide flexible funding to world-leading research groups addressing significant major research challenges. This funding opportunity is only for invited applicants. We will award 80% of the full economic costs (FEC) of the project.

grants.grant_title: Mathematical sciences postdoctoral fellowship
grants.funder_name: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council
grants.description: These are personal, career development focused awards for the mathematical sciences. You must have either: a PhD more than four years’ experience in a relevant field by the start of your fellowship Candidates with more research experience may prefer to apply for our open fellowship.

grants.grant_title: AHRC responsive mode: Curiosity Award
grants.description: Flexible awards to fund fundamental research that leads to new research agendas, networking activity and idea generation, which enables the development of further research opportunities and new research agendas.

grants.grant_title: AHRC responsive mode: catalyst awards
grants.description: AHRC awards to support researchers without prior experience of leading a significant research project to accelerate their trajectory as independent researchers, to unlock their potential and build leadership and convenor experience. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for AHRC funding.

grants.grant_title: AHRC responsive mode: UKRI NSF-SBE lead agency
grants.description: Apply for funding to work with overseas researchers in the Unites States (US). Submit a collaborative research grant application within the remit of AHRC and US National Science Foundation, Social, Behavioural and Economic Sciences Directorate (NSF-SBE). You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for AHRC funding.

grants.grant_title: AHRC responsive mode: working with Brazilian researchers
grants.description: Apply for funding to work with overseas researchers in the State of São Paulo, Brazil. Submit a collaborative research grant application within the remit of Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) and State of São Paulo Research Foundation (FAPESP). You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for AHRC funding.

grants.grant_title: AHRC responsive mode: collaborate with researchers in Luxembourg
grants.description: Apply for funding to work with overseas researchers in Luxembourg. Submit a collaborative research grant application within the remit of Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) and Fonds National de la Recherche (FNR). You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for AHRC funding.

grants.grant_title: AHRC responsive mode: standard research grant
grants.description: Apply for Arts and Humanities Research Council (AHRC) standard research grant funding to support well-defined collaborative projects across the arts and humanities, in areas covered by our remit. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for AHRC funding.

grants.grant_title: EPSRC programme grant outline stage
grants.funder_name: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council
grants.description: Programme grants provide flexible funding to world-leading UK research groups addressing significant research challenges. Please contact your relevant theme contact before applying. EPSRC will award 80% of the full economic costs of the project.

grants.grant_title: NERC urgency funding

grants.grant_title: Work with US-based researchers on environmental science: NERC-NSF lead agency

grants.grant_title: Collaborate with the functional genomics screening laboratory

grants.description: You can apply for a research grant in any area within the remit of Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC). You must be based at an eligible UK research organisation.

grants.grant_title: ESRC responsive mode: research grants round two
grants.description: This opportunity funds researcher-driven basic, applied, and strategic research from any disciplines and on any topics in ESRC’s remit, as long as the social sciences are more than 50% of the focus and effort. You can be based at any eligible UK research organisation to apply for ESRC funding.

grants.grant_title: ESRC responsive mode: new investigator grants round two
grants.description: This opportunity funds new researchers at the start of their careers to support them to become independent researchers through gaining experience of managing and leading research projects and teams. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for ESRC funding.

grants.grant_title: ESRC responsive mode: working with Luxembourg researchers round two
grants.description: This opportunity funds collaborative work with researchers in Luxembourg, governed by an agreement between UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) and FNR in Luxembourg. You may submit collaborative research proposals in any area of social sciences within the remit of both ESRC and FNR.

grants.grant_title: ESRC responsive mode: secondary data analysis round two
grants.description: Individuals and research teams can apply for funding to support high-impact, policy- and practitioner-relevant research through deeper exploitation of existing data resources from any disciplines and on any topics in ESRC’s remit. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for ESRC funding.

grants.grant_title: ESRC responsive mode: UKRI-SBE lead agency opportunity round two
grants.description: The UKRI-SBE lead agency funding opportunity allows UK and US-based researchers to submit a collaborative proposal that will go through a single review process. Grants are funded through existing funding programmes at the relevant lead agency. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for ESRC funding.

grants.grant_title: ESRC responsive mode: working with Brazilian researchers round two
grants.description: If you are a UK-based researcher you can apply for funding to work with overseas researchers in the State of São Paulo, Brazil. Submit collaborative research proposals in any area of the social sciences within the remit of both ESRC and FAPESP. You must be based at a UK research organisation eligible for ESRC funding.

grants.grant_title: Collaborate with researchers in Luxembourg
grants.description: UKRI and FNR have signed a memorandum of understanding to enable and support applications between researchers in the UK and Luxembourg. UKRI will fund the UK component and FNR will fund the Luxembourg component of the application. Conditions will differ depending on the funding opportunity.

grants.grant_title: Work with Brazilian researchers: NERC FAPESP lead agency
grants.description: This opportunity allows UK-based researchers and researchers in the State of São Paulo, Brazil to submit a collaborative application under existing NERC funding opportunities. This will go through a single review process.

grants.grant_title: PATT travel claim for competitively awarded observation time
grants.description: Claim back travel and subsistence costs when visiting an astronomy telescope. The visit must be for competitively awarded science observation time. You must be a UK-based researcher at an eligible UK institution. You must not already hold or have access to a travel grant from the Panel for the Allocation of Telescope Time (PATT).

grants.grant_title: Collaborate with researchers in Brazil
grants.description: Under the UKRI-FAPESP lead agency agreement, participant UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) research councils receive and assess joint applications from eligible UK and Brazil-based researchers from the state of São Paulo on behalf of both organisations.

grants.grant_title: UKRI-SBE lead agency opportunity
grants.description: The UKRI-SBE lead agency opportunity allows UK and US-based researchers to submit a collaborative proposal that will go through a single review process. Grants are funded through existing funding programmes at the relevant lead agency.

grants.grant_title: Daphne Jackson fellowship
grants.description: These fellowships are for those looking to return to a research career after a break. Fellowships combine a personalised retraining programme with a challenging research project. They are flexible, usually lasting three years at 0.5 full-time equivalent, although some UKRI funders may award longer.

grants.grant_title: Return to research support bursary
grants.description: You can apply for this bursary if you are a researcher looking to return to an academic research career after a break of two or more years. You can get funding of up to £2,500 for no more than six months to support you in developing a proposal suitable for submission to the STFC fellowship scheme.

grants.grant_title: Knowledge Transfer Partnership
grants.description: Knowledge Transfer Partnerships help businesses to partner with an academic organisation, research organisation or a Catapult, to employ a graduate with the skills and knowledge that can help the business to innovate and grow.

grants.grant_title: Researching ME/CFS: priority area
grants.description: Apply for funding to research myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome, also known as ME/CFS.

grants.grant_title: US-Pakistan Knowledge Corridor PhD Scholarship Program
grants.description: This scholarship program aims to award PhD scholarships to outstanding Pakistani students to study at top-ranked US universities. The initiative is designed to increase the quality of faculty in Pakistani Higher Education Institutions and enhance domestic research capacity.

grants.grant_title: Master Of Science (M.S.) Program
grants.funder_name: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
grants.description: A 36-credit graduate degree program designed to provide students with a solid foundation in their chosen scientific or engineering discipline. The program offers both thesis and non-thesis options and is typically completed in three semesters and one summer session.
grants.deadline_notes: APPLICATIONS FOR 2026 ARE OPEN

grants.grant_title: PhD Programme In Climate Change And Human Habitat (Cchh)
grants.funder_name: West African Science Service Centre on Climate Change and Adapted Land Use (WASCAL)
grants.description: A doctoral research programme hosted at the Federal University of Technology, Minna, Nigeria, designed to address the impacts of climate change in West Africa. The program focuses on developing sustainable cities, settlement dynamics, green infrastructure, and sustainable mobility.

grants.grant_title: Visiting Research Fellowship
grants.description: A visitor programme for academics and researchers to spend between three and twelve months at the Oxford Internet Institute. Fellows engage in collaborative, cross-disciplinary research on the Internet's most important issues.
grants.deadline_notes: Applications are accepted on an ongoing basis, but contact should be made with the department at least three months before any proposed visit to allow sufficient time for visa processing.

grants.grant_title: Technology And Geopolitics Fellowship
grants.funder_name: The Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs
grants.description: This fellowship supports pre- and post-doctoral fellows conducting research at the intersection of technology and geopolitics, focusing on areas like AI, biotechnology, quantum computing, and cyber challenges. Fellows are expected to work on their dissertations or publications while building relationships within the Harvard community.
grants.deadline_notes: The application period for the 2027–2028 academic year closes on December 1, 2026.

grants.grant_title: Advanced Grants
grants.description: Supports established research leaders with a proven track record to pursue ambitious, frontier research. Awards up to €2,500,000 for 5 years. Open to researchers of any nationality hosted at a European institution.

grants.grant_title: Research Grants
grants.description: Funds research and innovation to understand and address the causes and consequences of poverty in the UK. JRF supports a range of project scales and methods, with a focus on insights actionable for policy and practice.
grants.application_deadline_raw: Rolling — responsive to JRF programme priorities

grants.grant_title: Comenius Programme
grants.description: The Comenius programme contributes to the innovation and improvement of secondary vocational education and higher education in the Netherlands. It offers Teaching Fellow, Senior Fellow, and Leadership Fellow grants to enable education professionals to put their ideas for educational innovation into practice.

grants.grant_title: EIC Pathfinder
grants.description: Supports advanced research for visionary and radically new technologies with the potential to create new markets. Pathfinder Open accepts proposals from any field; consortium grants range from €3,000,000 to €4,000,000.

grants.grant_title: Proof of Concept Grants
grants.description: Enables ERC grant holders to explore the commercial or societal potential of their ERC-funded research results. Awards up to €150,000 for 18 months. Exclusively open to current or recent ERC grantees.

grants.grant_title: Civic Empowerment Grants
grants.description: Supports organisations working at the intersection of civic empowerment and data and digital rights. Luminate funds efforts to build transparent, accountable institutions and empower citizens to participate meaningfully in democratic processes.
grants.application_deadline_raw: Rolling — by invitation or open expression of interest

grants.grant_title: Investigator Awards in Science
grants.description: Supports outstanding researchers to pursue any direction of science that could significantly improve human or animal health. Awards cover salary, research costs, and indirect costs for 5–7 years.

grants.grant_title: Research Project Grants
grants.description: Supports innovative and original research projects across any academic discipline. Grants typically range from £100,000 to £500,000 for projects of up to 5 years. Four deadlines per year.

grants.grant_title: Research for Patient Benefit
grants.funder_name: National Institute for Health and Care Research
grants.description: Funds applied health and social care research with the potential to make a significant difference to NHS patients and service users. Awards up to £500,000 for up to 36 months. Three funding rounds per year.
grants.application_deadline_raw: Rolling — three funding rounds per year

grants.grant_title: Discovery Awards
grants.description: Funds established research groups to pursue ambitious, long-term programmes with transformative potential for human or animal health. Awards range from £500,000 to £5,000,000 over up to 8 years.

grants.grant_title: Grand Challenges Explorations
grants.description: Funds innovative ideas in global health and development. Initial awards of $100,000 with high-performing projects eligible for follow-on grants exceeding $1,000,000. Open to applicants from any discipline worldwide.
grants.application_deadline_raw: Rolling — multiple thematic rounds per year

grants.grant_title: Kiem Gofood
grants.description: The aim of KIEM GoFood is to conduct (explorative) applied research through new practice-oriented research activities on two specific themes within the Knowledge and Innovation Agenda Agriculture Water Food.
grants.deadline_notes: Upcoming submission date

grants.grant_title: HORIZON-JU-EDCTP3-2026-Scientific Leadership Man SSA Prize
Eu Mission: A Soil Deal For Europe
grants.description: Under Horizon Europe, the European Commission funds research and innovation projects to restore and protect the health of soils in Europe and beyond. The program aims to ensure safe food and water, ecosystems, and resilience towards natural disasters.
grants.deadline_notes: Single-stage proposal submission deadline is 23 September 2026. Two-stage second stage submission deadline is 15 September 2026. (First stage closed on 14 April 2026).
